themes.exampleQuote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
there were a lot of good options for drinks! both drinks my sister and I tried were delicious.
I just wish this place was bigger, gets packed quickly!
the workers are so nice every time I visit!
I asked for a blueberry muffin, received a raisin/walnut muffin — I had to pay for a new muffin.
The wi-fi isn't good enough to work from here so I wouldn't come back for that.

notable_quote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently single out the specialty drinks — the strawberry matcha and iced tiramisu latte in particular — and the warmth of the staff, while the most pointed criticism comes from a single service error that cost a customer money and a finding that the wi-fi can't support remote work.
quarter_narrative: Delanie's Coffee Shadyside held an 82% five-star rate across 104 Google reviews and drew a fresh review as recently as two days ago, signaling a loyal, returning customer base and a room that stays busy enough to keep generating opinions. Where it lags is in the channels that build discovery beyond the immediate neighborhood: Instagram produced three posts and zero reels in the last 30 days, with the account going quiet for nearly two weeks, a thin output for a shop whose specialty drinks are the kind of visually arresting thing that circulates. In the Pittsburgh Cafes family, Allegheny Coffee & Tea Exchange leads this issue largely on social momentum — a signal Delanie's hasn't yet turned on. The path upward in the rankings runs directly through Instagram frequency; the review foundation and the creator-fit signal suggest the raw material is already there.
tldr_read: Exceptional creator fit and a locked conversion path, undercut by a dormant Instagram — Ones to Watch, #4 in Pittsburgh Coffee Shops.
tldr_meaning: The review engine is running and the profile infrastructure is complete; the rank ceiling is currently set by an Instagram cadence that produced three posts and no reels in the past 30 days.

playbook.headline: Post reels around your signature drinks
playbook.action: Zero reels in 30 days is the single gap between a 96 creator-fit score and actual discovery — start with the strawberry matcha.

playbook.headline: Build out the Google photo catalog
playbook.action: 96 photos across 10 categories sits below the family median of 77 visual score — add dedicated shots of the seasonal menu board and baked goods case.

playbook.headline: Tighten the order-correction policy
playbook.action: One documented case of charging a customer for a staff error is the most concrete service complaint in the review set — a clear remake protocol removes it as a recurring risk.
